scope_note: QA Induction Motor Slip cert [307]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5; Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions: Rational Trigonometry, Wild Egg Books, ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8; Chapman, S.J. (2011) Electric Machinery Fundamentals, 5th ed., McGraw-Hill, ISBN 978-0-07-352954-7. CLAIM: (C1) Grade alternation = versor phase lag: det(M^k)=(-1)^k (Cassini cert [299]); slip=k/24 as Fraction; odd k=reactive (lagging) versor, even k=active rotor; k=0 synchronous, k=12 antipodal M^12=-I (cert [298]); verified k=0..23. (C2) Spread profile non-trivial: s_k=(b0*e_k-e0*b_k)^2/(G0*G_k) Fraction-valued for k=0..23; s_0=0 (synchronous), s_12=0 (antipodal, parallel direction); all other s_k in (0,1)cap Q; not monotone. (C3) Pullout T-step: k*=22 (from seed (1,1)); s(k*)=16/41; symmetry s_22=s_23=16/41 (T^22=(1,9) and T^23=(9,1) give maximum directional deviation from seed). (C4) Orbit symmetry: s_12=0 because M^12 equiv -I maps (1,1) to (8,8)=8*(1,1) mod 9 (scalar multiple, cert [298]); two s=0 points {k=0,k=12} partition orbit into two half-cycles. (C5) Theorem NT: slip k/24, torque proxy s_k/s_max, rotor speed 1-k/24 are observer projections (Fraction from integer orbit, never re-entering T-step); Kloss curve T=2T_max/(s/s*+s*/s) is observer layer. Builds on cert [298] (M^12=-I), cert [299] (Cassini det), cert [305] (rational spread power factor). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
